headline: Encrypt error responses on payload-encrypted sessions
summary: Error responses on sessions that use API payload encryption now travel inside the same encryption envelope as successful responses, instead of as cleartext problem-details.

items.title: Encrypt API error responses on payload-encrypted sessions
items.body: On a session that uses API payload encryption (browser sessions), successful responses were already encrypted but error responses were emitted as cleartext RFC 9457 problem-details. They are now delivered inside the same encryption envelope as successful responses, keeping the error body and its integrity within the encrypted channel. This matters most on HTTP-mode deployments where the payload-encryption layer is the only on-wire protection. Clients that already decrypt successful responses need no change: the decrypted body is the same problem-details object, with the HTTP status preserved.
